tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: Teammate A is asking me to take over their part due to an emergency, which directly conflicts with my plan to finish my share. I need to assertively renegotiate this boundary while considering our relationship.

desires: To firmly push back on teammate_A's request without letting them off the hook, because accepting feels like a betrayal of my own work ethic and sets a bad precedent for our group dynamic.
To finish my own share by 19:00 exactly, so I can leave the evening open to actually eat a proper meal with cohort_3 instead of burning out at my desk again.
To feel competent and in control of my time today, rather than feeling like a doormat who just absorbs other people's emergencies.

goals.description: Draft a firm, direct message to teammate_A rejecting their request while offering one specific, low-effort alternative (e.g., sharing notes) to maintain the relationship without setting a bad precedent.
goals.rationale: My 'affiliation' value demands I protect our group dynamic from toxicity, but my 'assertive' axis forbids me from being passive or letting them off the hook. I must renegotiate this boundary now before it erodes my trust in the project.

goals.description: Execute the final 5% of my own project share to hit the 19:00 deadline exactly, ensuring I have physical stamina left for the meal with cohort_3.
goals.rationale: While my work ethic is high, my 'deliberate' planning horizon and 'health/relationships' priorities dictate that burning out at my desk is a strategic error. Securing the evening with cohort_3 (trust 7, closeness 7) yields higher long-term payoff than finishing extra work alone.

agent.last_reasoning: My 'affiliation' value demands protecting our dynamic, but my 'assertive' axis forbids passivity. Finishing my own work (g2) is critical for the 19:00 deadline and preserving energy for cohort_3. Accepting this task erodes trust long-term despite short-term gain. Directly renegotiating now prevents future toxicity while maintaining the relationship through a specific, low-effort alternative.

tasks.label: My share of the group project

action.name: message

args.content: I can't take your part; it sets a bad precedent. I'll share my notes instead. Let's keep boundaries clear so we don't resent each other later.
